headline: School Meal Program Budget of RM3.50 Per Student Deemed Inadequate
context: The Rancangan Makanan Tambahan allocates only RM3.50 per meal per student despite food cost inflation since 2020.

cards.big: School meal budget of RM3.50 per student has not changed since 2020 despite 25% food inflation
cards.sub: 850,000 B40 students receive meals that caterers struggle to produce at budget

cards.big: Nutritional analysis of RMT meals shows 40% fail to meet minimum caloric requirements
cards.sub: Budget constraints force caterers to reduce portions and protein content

cards.big: MOE awarded 2,300 meal contracts to lowest bidders with minimal nutrition auditing
cards.sub: Price-based procurement sacrifices nutritional quality for cost savings

cards.big: Brazil's school meal program spends equivalent RM7.20 per student with mandatory local sourcing
cards.sub: Higher investment plus local procurement improves both nutrition and farmer incomes

cards.big: Feeding hungry children poorly is almost as harmful as not feeding them at all
Should school meal budgets be indexed to food price inflation with nutritional minimum standards
